# Individual Development Plan

Name: Yi Chen  
Role: Staff Software Engineer

Status: Draft

Still draft, work-in-progress.

## Key Strengths:

*What are your superpowers at work? Start with your strengths to help identify other areas where you should focus your development and drive more meaningful growth.*

* Set and drive the technical roadmap and help product and engineering managers make good tradeoffs in the roadmap and team allocation (see [senior staff engineer level expectations](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1P-IIwNYgIy715I3qaws0KEOipnvd27_6i7tYdyjN7C0/edit#bookmark=id.6efbc93ent9m)).   
  * In late Feb 2024, one month after joining Slack,   
    * I identified using spot instances for our Spark infrastructure was the biggest root cause for reliability and cost challenges. I put together an analysis document named [Spot or Not](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1J9AwR2G_UM10IvGB2bZp_TefA6DZU_D2rgCvtgUi0UM/edit#), and  [shared](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CK4C1AWNA/p1708373418321719) it to the \#data-design-docs for feedback. I received extremely positive feedback, and within a week (from Feb 21, 2024), this became the top priority and I started the execution of removing spot instances from data-platform clusters.  
    * In Feb, I identified that the lack of YARN and Spark metrics can be a blocking issue for improving both Spark reliability and cost efficiency. I [proposed and drove the reprioritization](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qPAvq4bMi5fzmSpqC9D4z0E8GtAS9QCJe5SmaJQQvTw/edit#) of these two projects in March.   
  * In March 2024:  
    * I started working on the road map for EMR Reliability and Cost  
    * Identifying the lack of YARN and Spark metrics is a bottleneck for helping the team to debug infra-level issues and to make data-driven decisions. [Shared the technical design with the team](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C8EDM9MGA/p1711721270859669?thread_ts=1711576764.302179&cid=C8EDM9MGA) to speed up the implementation. Set the road map and priority of these projects by creating  [a high-level proposal and shared with the team](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qPAvq4bMi5fzmSpqC9D4z0E8GtAS9QCJe5SmaJQQvTw/edit#heading=h.lnqfcyy21mit). As a result, the DI team met on March 31 to discuss this proposal and decided to prioritize these projects as high priority projects in Q2 ( [Rahul Gidwani](mailto:rgidwani@salesforce.com)as the DRI for Spark metrics, and [Shreedhar Pawar](mailto:shreedhar.pawar@salesforce.com)as the DRI for the YARN metrics). The outcome is that both projects were prioritized for FY25Q2, and both were delivered in the beginning of Q3, unlocking new cost saving opportunities (thanks for [Rahul Gidwani](mailto:rgidwani@salesforce.com)to implement the Spark metrics project, and [Gage Gaskins](mailto:ggaskins@salesforce.com)to quickly finish up the YARN metrics project)  
    * I took over the planning and road map effort in March, proposed to work on both reliability and cost at the same time, due to the feedback from key stakeholders that in the past we prioritized cost reduction using spot instances which hurt reliability. I shared the [new road map for Spark reliability and efficiency](https://docs.google.com/document/d/14-xglnAQ4woT7xkQuMb5TMH7wbDplgWhYfFQLjXUZMA/edit) with the team and key stakeholders. As a result, [from March to July, we have saved $6.21 million annual savings for EMR cost](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C8F0SQNSH/p1722537988236069), and at the same time [reduced the reliability related escals from key users by  71%, and reliability related escals from all EMR users by 65%](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C06G45LGG7M/p1723138414787169).   
    * Proposed and prioritized Automated and continuous  Health Monitor ([slack](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1714077036830369))  
  * In April  
    * [Identified livy is a major reliability risk](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C06USAK1LAV/p1713362503575499?thread_ts=1713288613.964659&cid=C06USAK1LAV) for Spark on EMR by root-cause analysis of incident \#incd-240415-51164-metrics-foundations-emr-jobs-are-failing and a few other escals (See [escal](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1712936476087089), [escal](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1713162996251129)). This helped prioritize projects to improve Livy’s reliability, and added this to [the road map](https://docs.google.com/document/d/14-xglnAQ4woT7xkQuMb5TMH7wbDplgWhYfFQLjXUZMA/edit#bookmark=id.wm7idhaln89t)  by recommending [replacing Livy with Genie](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1712947948040379?thread_ts=1712936476.087089&cid=C01FB307Z7A) integration for the short-term. Livy related reliability issues are not a concern as of July 2024 (thanks for [Gage Gaskins](mailto:ggaskins@salesforce.com)major technical contribution including HDFS caching and Livy/Quarry improvements). As a result, Livy risk has been resolved by July ([analysis result](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vEQ5UD_r-cnqmI33CBxIDprPv074OHdYe_09wiZZeMY/edit#)).   
    * I identified the lack of a continuous EMR health check as a high priority challenge for the team from an S1 incident and other escals that can be avoided with automation. It is also crucial to have reliability metrics that are [decoupled from the job-level reliability metrics](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C06S0A30LCT/p1714069179001929?thread_ts=1713889907.126709&cid=C06S0A30LCT). [I proposed this project with a design](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1714077036830369). As a result, [Gage Gaskins](mailto:ggaskins@salesforce.com)has implemented this project with interesting new information that we are not aware of.   
  * May \- June  
    * [Identified shuffle reliability risk](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C8EDM9MGA/p1713899549799059) from escal-data, an issue that the team usually categorizes as a spot instance issue. I started [tracking and monitoring the risk](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C8EDM9MGA/p1715628464104299) from May, and did [a thorough analysis](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xkddnyMmSJOyVHAAv8oMUll4Bw3KJo1TxCWDEnil3KU/edit#) June, started the [discussion with the team and prioritized this project as high priority for Spark reliability](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1718146768066259). Proposed a few short-term fixes and a long term solution (by adopting Apache Celeborn). As a result, (1) I estimated that Celeborn adoption can potentially save $800k/year, [Rahul Gidwani](mailto:rgidwani@salesforce.com)will be the DRI for Celeborn adoption and is planned with a high priority project in the coming quarters, and (2) I implemented the short-term solutions and as of July 2024, shuffle reliability risk is largely resolved ([analysis result](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vEQ5UD_r-cnqmI33CBxIDprPv074OHdYe_09wiZZeMY/edit#)).   
  * July \- August  
    * Analyzed [Spark infrastructure reliability improvements](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vEQ5UD_r-cnqmI33CBxIDprPv074OHdYe_09wiZZeMY/edit#) since I joined Slack, with evidence showing significant reduction of reliability related escals, showing 71% reduction in reliability related escals from dma and data-platform clusters, and 65% reduction unreliability related escals from all EMR clusters. This set the stage for the new phase of reliability and cost project at Slack.

* Have detailed conversations about the architecture and design of multiple systems and codebases  
  * Proposed [Continuous HealthCheck high-level desigh Design, implemented by Gage Gaskins](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1714077036830369)  
  * Proposed [YARN and Spark Metrics high-level design Design](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C8EDM9MGA/p1711721270859669?thread_ts=1711576764.302179&cid=C8EDM9MGA), implemented by [Rahul Gidwani](mailto:rgidwani@salesforce.com)and [Gage Gaskins](mailto:ggaskins@salesforce.com)  
* Works with many cross-functional peers (design, CE, finance, business operations, or marketing) to bring clarity to not just engineering but Slack as a whole.  
  * I have worked closely with [Emily Ferron](mailto:efarron@salesforce.com)from the Finance team since February. Since the beginning of the EMR Cost project in March, I have been validating with the cost savings before publishing the result to the \#announce-data channel. Provide detailed answers to her about temporary cost increases (e.g. ODCR wastage) and keep her in the loop about the road map and plans.  
  *   
* Both build prototypes to prove long-term technical or product strategy and builds a culture of prototyping and experimentation within the teams.  
  * For the EMR Cost Saving project, after coming up with a [plan](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1J9AwR2G_UM10IvGB2bZp_TefA6DZU_D2rgCvtgUi0UM/edit), I started working on one cluster (data-platform clusters), [prototyped and validated every single proposal, and took detailed notes on the results](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Q8dkvYVi6Fwxs22Ta2Ei-8GwBia54cn6fyOgWB_hIW4/edit#heading=h.lnqfcyy21mit). This becomes the blueprint for the subsequent cluster-level optimization effort.  
* Beacon of technical rigor and quality in implementation.  
  * The DI team has faced  developed learned helplessness for reliability challenges. As a mostly a dev-ops team for EMR, I encouraged the team to root-cause each issue when possible, instead of kicking the can down the road by re-provisioning the cluster for fast resolution. As a result, most root causes are fixed by July, Spark/EMR are mostly reliable, which changes the team culture and mindset from being dev-ops to being engineers who take ownership of products.  
    * Examples:  
      *  [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1708379660577459?thread\_ts=1708374215.725459\&cid=C01FB307Z7A](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1708379660577459?thread_ts=1708374215.725459&cid=C01FB307Z7A)  
      * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CNSBT36CQ/p1711468819558079?thread\_ts=1711391766.369869\&cid=CNSBT36CQ](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CNSBT36CQ/p1711468819558079?thread_ts=1711391766.369869&cid=CNSBT36CQ)  
      * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1711557571802619?thread\_ts=1711555883.837969\&cid=C01FB307Z7A](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1711557571802619?thread_ts=1711555883.837969&cid=C01FB307Z7A)  
      * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1715018368001089?thread\_ts=1715014490.441329\&cid=CJFHE7AR3](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1715018368001089?thread_ts=1715014490.441329&cid=CJFHE7AR3)  
      * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C0709GZBD4G/p1724364726191649?thread\_ts=1724354276.461609\&cid=C0709GZBD4G](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C0709GZBD4G/p1724364726191649?thread_ts=1724354276.461609&cid=C0709GZBD4G)  
  * I directly contributed to several incidents demonstrating how to root-cause complicated issues.  
  * I demonstrated and corrected a few misconceptions the team has regarding EMR, which led to the assumption that EMR was the reason for the reliability challenges we have been facing.  
    * Examples: The team thought EMR autoscaling is too aggressive.  
      * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1718039880205859?thread\_ts=1716311473.633149\&cid=C01FB307Z7A](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1718039880205859?thread_ts=1716311473.633149&cid=C01FB307Z7A)  
      * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1715635963264749?thread\_ts=1715628074.044779\&cid=C01FB307Z7A](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/C01FB307Z7A/p1715635963264749?thread_ts=1715628074.044779&cid=C01FB307Z7A)  
  * The over-all result was increased customer satisfaction, trust for the team. See [Nikhil Rajendra](mailto:nrajendra@salesforce.com)’s direct feedback [here](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/D06GLJ9QVMJ/p1712172946290919).

![][image1]

* Comfortably communicates complex issues to diverse audiences. Inspires discussions that uncover new problems or novel solutions.  
  *   
* Proactively identifies and remedies communication gaps and issues to reach alignment.   
* Trusted coach and mentor, actively building new leaders at Slack. Sponsor and elevate underrepresented talent across engineering teams.  
  * I’ve been asked by multiple teams and managers to mentor them and their teams.   
*  Proactively identifies and remedies communication gaps and issues to reach alignment.   
  * Backfill reliability.  
  * Datahub listener reliability  
* Expert at giving feedback, modeling a healthy feedback culture, and fostering development by providing it continuously.   
  * [https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1717718393974509?thread\_ts=1717200136.108029\&cid=CJFHE7AR3](https://slack-pde.slack.com/archives/CJFHE7AR3/p1717718393974509?thread_ts=1717200136.108029&cid=CJFHE7AR3)  
  *   
* Consistently works through abstract problems that require analysis of complicated variables.   
  * Translate vague business requirements of improving reliability and reduce cost into actionable plans.  
*  Identifies and evaluates fundamental issues, providing strategy and direction for major functional areas.  
  * Shuffle reliability

    

    

    ## Short-Term Development Goals 

* *identify your top two development goals for the next 12 months.*  
  * *Write them as the outcome you’re trying to achieve.*  
  * *Examples: Improve my impact presenting to large audiences.*  
  * *Ensure that your goals are specific, measurable, attainable, relevant, and time-bound ([SMART](https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/y31jQdRKDWFZ5wzXh1LTaj)).*   
* *determine specific actions you can take to achieve your development goals.*  
  * *Development actions should come from a variety of sources (check out the [70-20-10 model](https://www.growthengineering.co.uk/70-20-10-model/)) — hands-on experiences, collaborating with a coach or mentor, or participating in a class/educational offering.*  
  * *When listing the actions you want to take, make sure to set a deadline for yourself, and also identify where you may need help from your manager or others to achieve them.* 

    

    ## Long-Term Development Goals

* *consider your career goals and aspirations over the next 2 – 3 years.*  
  * *It may help to think about the types of roles you want to move into next as you determine the direction you want to drive your career.*

    

    

    Notes from: [https://slack.enterprise.slack.com/docs/T024BE7LD/F0711MENHSL](https://slack.enterprise.slack.com/docs/T024BE7LD/F0711MENHSL)

    

    ## **Four Steps to Build Your IDP**

    You have your template in Org62, and now it’s time to build *your* unique roadmap to reach those development goals.

* **Key Strengths \-** think about characteristics, behaviors or skills where you thrive and feel most energized.  
  * Start with your strengths to help identify other areas where you should focus your development and drive more meaningful growth.  
  * Examples: *Strong project management skills; expert at agile development methodology; excellent managing through ambiguity; effective communication; coaching and developing; data analysis to storytelling; customer engagement/relationship building.*  
* **Short-Term Development Goals \-** identify your top two development goals for the next 12 months.  
  * Write them as the outcome you’re trying to achieve.  
  * Examples: *Improve my impact presenting to large audiences.*  
  * Ensure that your goals are specific, measurable, attainable, relevant, and time-bound ([SMART](https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/y31jQdRKDWFZ5wzXh1LTaj)).  
* **Add Objectives \-** determine specific actions you can take to achieve your development goals.  
  * Development actions should come from a variety of sources *(check out the [70-20-10 model](https://www.growthengineering.co.uk/70-20-10-model/))* — hands-on experiences, collaborating with a coach or mentor, or participating in a class/educational offering.  
  * When listing the actions you want to take, make sure to set a deadline for yourself, and also identify where you may need help from your manager or others to achieve them.  
* **Long-Term Development Goals \-** consider your career goals and aspirations over the next 2 – 3 years.  
  * It may help to think about the *types of roles* you want to move into next as you determine the direction you want to drive your career.
